articles.title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
articles.description:
“You know, it’s tiring. My mouth, honestly, couldn’t feel worse. But I’ll get some work. We’ve got a really good dentist here, who’s great. I’m really happy we got two points tonight.”

The post Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Charlie McAvoy scored the winning goal for the Bruins on March 10.
McAvoy expressed discomfort but praised the team's dentist for his care.
The Bruins secured two crucial points in the standings for Boston.

articles.title: Maine’s catch of lobster declines again as high costs and climate change impact industry
articles.description:
The haul of lobsters, Maine's best known export and a key piece of the state's identity and culture, has declined every year since 2021, and some scientists have cited as a reason warming oceans that spur migration to Canadian waters.

The post Maine’s catch of lobster declines again as high costs and climate change impact industry appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maine's lobster catch has declined annually since 2021 due to climate change.
Scientists report warming oceans are driving lobsters to Canadian waters.
Boston's seafood industry may face shortages and increased prices as a result.

articles.title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
articles.description:
Jaylen Brown had eight points and seven rebounds in 15 minutes at the time of his ejection.

The post Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jaylen Brown was ejected during the Spurs game on March 10, 2026.
He recorded eight points and seven rebounds in just 15 minutes before the ejection.
Brown's criticism of refs may influence future officiating in Celtics games.

articles.title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
articles.description:
Angel Alvarez, 68, was sentenced to life in prison with the possibility of parole after 17 years, prosecutors said.

The post Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Angel Alvarez, 68, received a life sentence for stabbing a Chelsea woman, 59.
Alvarez is eligible for parole after serving 17 years in prison.
The case highlights ongoing concerns about violence in the Chelsea community.
